input_candidates.truth_value: κ-carbide precipitates first from austenitic matrix. Cu atoms are rejected from κ-carbide, enriching the surrounding matrix. When Cu concentration reaches critical level, Cu clusters form and precipitate as Cu-rich phase. Cu-rich phase nucleates in austenitic matrix near κ-carbide due to lower misfit with matrix (1.0%) compared to with κ-carbide (3.4%). The presence of κ-carbide promotes Cu-rich phase precipitation. Addition of Cu increases austenite lattice parameter, promoting κ-carbide precipitation.
input_candidates.prediction_value: κ-carbide precipitates preferentially than Cu-rich phase, Cu atoms are excluded from κ-carbide and accumulate around κ-carbide, then Cu-rich phase precipitates when Cu concentration reaches critical value. κ-carbide and Cu-rich phase have cube-on-cube orientation relationship with austenitic matrix: [11\-0]γ//[11\-0]κ//[110]Cu, (11\-1)γ//(11\-1)κ//(11\-1)Cu.

input_candidates.truth_value: Cu addition to Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C steel increases the lattice parameter of austenitic matrix (from 0.3687 nm to 0.3689 nm), which promotes precipitation of κ-carbide.
input_candidates.prediction_value: Adding Cu to Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C austenitic low-density steel can increase the lattice parameter of austenitic matrix, promote the precipitation of κ-carbide, and κ-carbide can also promote the precipitation of Cu-rich phase.

input_candidates.truth_value: The precipitation of κ-carbide expelled Cu atoms, locally increasing Cu concentration in surrounding matrix; upon reaching critical concentration, Cu clusters form and precipitate as Cu-rich phase.
input_candidates.prediction_value: The critical lattice parameter of austenitic matrix for κ-carbide precipitation is about 0.3670 nm, lattice parameter lower than this value will not form intragranular κ-carbide.

input_candidates.truth_value: The critical lattice parameter of austenitic matrix for κ-carbide precipitation is approximately a = 0.3670 nm; below this, κ-carbide cannot precipitate. The calculation suggests a composition boundary formula exists for intragranular κ-carbide precipitation in Fe-Mn-Al-C steels.
